source_pr.title: planner: fix update plan's projection elimination will cause column resolution error.
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: AilinKid <314806019@qq.com>

<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/37568

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?
the update plan's projection elimination will cause column resolution error in the plan optimizing phase.

the reason is:
update's assignment expression depends on the old projection output. (there is an implicit column reference there, so we couldn't just do the projection elimination there independently)

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
fix update plan's projection elimination will cause column resolution error.
```

symptoms: tk.MustExec("update tb1 set tb1.b=(select tb2.b from tb2 where tb2.a=tb1.a order by c desc limit 1);") (planner/core/cbo_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustExec("update tb1 set tb1.b=(select tb2.b from tb2 where tb2.a=tb1.a order by c desc limit 1);") (planner/core/cbo_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustExec("update tb1 set tb1.b=(select tb2.b from tb2 where tb2.a=tb1.a order by c desc limit 1);") (planner/core/cbo_test.go)
